title: Issues are not a gap-sibling first-class open-work kind — a task cannot focus on / address an issue as it can a gap
description: Framework-gaps are first-class open work in the PM model: task_addresses_gap relates a task to a gap, the task-completed-gap-closed invariant enforces closure, and framework-gaps is a next_ranked open-work kind in state_derivation so currentState surfaces open gaps as focus / next-actions. Issues have no parity — there is no task_addresses_issue relation (issues are reached only via decision_addresses_issue / feature_resolves_issue / gap_relates_to_issue), no issue-closure invariant, and issues are not a next_ranked kind, so currentState never surfaces an open issue as focusable next work. User-verbatim requirement: "we need gaps and issues to be sibling class items throughout the substrate. a task can and must be able to focus as they can with gaps on issues." currentState (context-sdk.ts:733-961) and lens-view are already kind-general (FEAT-004 / TASK-020), so the asymmetry is purely in config/catalog vocabulary (relation_types, invariants, state_derivation) plus two additive issue-schema lifecycle fields — not in code.

evidence.reference: task_addresses_gap (tasks->framework-gaps) present; there is no task_addresses_issue — a task cannot address an issue the way it addresses a gap
task-completed-gap-closed present; no issue analog (e.g. task-completed-issue-resolved), so completing a task against an issue is not lifecycle-checked
framework-gaps is a next_ranked open-work kind; issues is absent, so currentState never ranks/focuses an open issue
currentState is kind-general (reads state_derivation); confirms the asymmetry is config/catalog vocabulary, not deriver code
issues lacks resolved_at and an x-lifecycle block that framework-gaps has — the schema-side lifecycle-parity delta
the gap-explore investigation: the full five-axis asymmetry map, the required-vs-acceptable-divergence determination, and the prior-art search (genuinely unfiled)

impact: Issues are filed but invisible to the derived focus / what's-next surface and not closeable-by-task in the tracked model — they are second-class relative to gaps. issue-004 (and every issue) sits outside the task-driven close loop that gaps enjoy: no task can focus on or address it, and its resolution is not lifecycle-checked. Sibling parity is required for issues to participate in derivation, focus, and task-closure exactly as gaps do.
canonical_vocabulary: first-class sibling block-kind / open-work parity (kind-general PM derivation + relation/invariant parity)
proposed_resolution: Per the investigation, group the required set: (a) add relation_types task_addresses_issue + issue_relates_to_issue (the reverse issue_addressed_by_* and the decision_raises_issue / missing-convention / escalation analogs are NOT required — gaps are the escalation sink by design, an acceptable divergence); (b) add invariant task-completed-issue-resolved (mirror of task-completed-gap-closed, severity warning); (c) add a state_derivation.next_ranked issues entry (bucket todo, rank_field priority, rank_order critical->low) — no deriver code change, since issues.status open already buckets to todo; (d) two additive issue-schema fields resolved_at + an x-lifecycle block; (e) issues-by-status + issues-by-priority lenses, and catalog back-port (samples/conception.json) of every new relation_type / invariant / lens. The catalog back-port leg interacts with FGAP-094: a new live relation_type absent from the packaged catalog makes a fresh accept-all substrate reject its edge at write. Status-label / priority-scale / category-facet divergences between the two schemas are acceptable kind-specific differences and are NOT force-symmetrized.
